loss_record.structural_divergence: tagged-union macro family replaces native sum type: multiple declarations (enum discriminator + union field with two arms + wrapper struct) replace a single ADT constructor; arms encoded as integer tag values (name_OK, name_ERR) instead of named constructors; pattern matching becomes switch(res.tag) with manual exhaustiveness; instantiation requires caller-supplied type and macro-name identifiers
loss_record.domain_narrowing: C cannot statically enforce exhaustiveness over the two arms; the realization narrows the abstraction domain to programs where the caller manually checks res.tag before accessing res.v; programs that omit the check are in the narrowed-out domain
loss_record.ub_introduction: accessing res.v.ok when res.tag == name_ERR or res.v.err when res.tag == name_OK reads an uninitialised union field; this is undefined behaviour in C; the abstraction statically excludes these states; the C realization introduces UB on exactly those states
loss_record.domain_narrowing_no_static_checked_excep: Rust Result<T,E> provides static exhaustiveness checking via match expressions; C tagged unions cannot enforce this; the realization domain is narrowed to C programs where the programmer manually enforces exhaustiveness at every match site
